articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Raphinha brace powers Brazil to 4-0 rout over Peru
article.description: Raphinha converted a penalty in each half to guide an improved Brazil to a 4-0 home win over Peru in South American World Cup qualifiers on Tuesday.
article.linkText: Raphinha brace carries Brazil to 4-0 WCQ win

article.story:
Raphinha converted a penalty in each half to guide an improved Brazil to a 4-0 home win over Peru in South American World Cup qualifiers on Tuesday.

Building on last week's 2-1 comeback win in Chile, Brazil looked livelier from the start and dominated possession, with late goals from Andreas Pereira and Luiz Henrique putting the gloss on an impressive performance.

Brazil, who had lost four of five qualifiers before their win in Chile on Thursday, are fourth in the standings on 16 points after 10 games, trailing third-placed Uruguay on goal difference.

"I think we needed this, it was really important winning both games to get back on track," Raphinha told Brazilian television Globo.

"They were two games that we managed to control. It's a long way from perfect, but I believe we're on the right track."

After hitting the crossbar in the 24th minute, Raphinha gave the home side the lead from the penalty spot in the 38th minute after Peru defender Carlos Zambrano was penalised for handball.

The Barcelona forward extended Brazil's lead from the spot in the 54th minute after Zambrano had fouled Savinho in the box.

Substitute Pereira scored Brazil's third with an acrobatic volley in the 72nd minute, brilliantly finishing a cross from Luiz Henrique, who wrapped up the win two minutes later with a low strike from the edge of the box.

Argentina hammered Bolivia 6-0 and top the standings on 22 points, three points ahead of Colombia, who thrashed last-placed Chile 4-0.

Davinson Sanchez opened the scoring for Colombia with a deflected header in the 34th minute and Luis Diaz doubled their lead early in the second half with an easy finish into an empty net after a bad mistake by the visiting side's defence.

Jhon Duran and Luis Sinisterra wrapped up the win as Chile suffered a fifth consecutive defeat in qualifying.

Paraguay beat Venezuela 2-1 and are sixth on 13 points, level with fifth-placed Ecuador and one ahead of Bolivia in seventh. The top six sides are guaranteed a berth at the 2026 finals in North America.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Bryan Reyna (Peru) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Brazil 1, Peru 0. Raphinha (Brazil) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Pedro Gallese (Peru) is shown the yellow card.
Jesús Castillo (Peru) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Brazil 2, Peru 0. Raphinha (Brazil) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Carlos Zambrano (Peru) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Peru. Oliver Sonne replaces Jesús Castillo.
Substitution, Peru. Luis Ramos replaces Bryan Reyna.
Wilder Cartagena (Peru) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Brazil. Andreas Pereira replaces Rodrygo.
Substitution, Brazil. Luiz Henrique replaces Savinho.
Goal! Brazil 3, Peru 0. Andreas Pereira (Brazil) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Luiz Henrique with a cross.
Substitution, Peru. Joao Grimaldo replaces Édison Flores.
Substitution, Peru. Andy Polo replaces Luis Advíncula.
Goal! Brazil 4, Peru 0. Luiz Henrique (Brazil) left footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Igor Jesus.
Substitution, Brazil. Matheus Pereira replaces Raphinha.
Substitution, Brazil. Endrick replaces Igor Jesus.
Substitution, Peru. Jorge Murrugarra replaces Sergio Peña.
Substitution, Brazil. André replaces Bruno Guimarães because of an injury.
Vanderson (Brazil) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, Brazil 4, Peru 0.
